mechanics.text:
TRAITS
ENEMY (multiple trait) Value: 1-3 The character has attracted a enemy; either single per- son Or group. The trait is worth 1 2 or 3 points depending on the power and influence of the enemy in comparison t0 the char- acter and the organization he is part of. 1-point enemy is about as powerful as the character, while 2-point enemy more powerful than the character and 3-point enemy is much more powerful than the character. The  Sample Contacts (P_ 208) can also serve as sample enemies since people likely to be valuable friends are also the kind of people that make dangerous adversaries; Enemies can provide useful inspiration for the gamemas ter'$ campaign and can serve as excellent motivators for the characters_ Enemies should get involved adventures often enough to be nuisance. but they should not dominate the group plot  lines- After all, only one character took on the Enemy Trait; so that character shouldn make the whole group pay for it; If, however, the gamemaster planned on having cer- tain enemy act as nemesis for the entire group through much of the campaign_ all the characters the group could take tne same Enemy Trait to represent it.
averaging 1.7 meters tall and often weighing no more than 50 kilograms_ Efforts increase perceptive  ability and reaction times have had the side effect of enlarged craniums and eyes, giving pilots very distinctive appearance that sometimes caus es them t0 be shunned if they wash out of training; where they work most often in the scientist or technician castes The primary advantages pilots receive are modified Attribute thresholds in the Life Path (STR -1= BOD -1 DEX +2 RFL +21 and  modified Attribute maximums as noted on the Attribute Maximums  Table 56). Because they have been carefully designed and bred over dozens of generations to fly aerospace fighters pilots are considered t0 have the Natural Aptitude Trait (p. 84) for all the skills in the Clan Fighter Pilot Field In addition all pilots have the G-Tolerance and Glass Jaw traits (below) at no Character Point cost/value
G-TOLERANCE Cost: character with this trait is especially resistant t0 the stress of high G maneuvering, most often found in fighter craft and accelerating /decelerating Drop Ships All target numbers that relate to the effects of high gravity have -2 TN modifier for G-Tolerant characters_
EXCEPTIONAL ATTRIBUTE (multiple tralt) Cost: This entitles the character to have one Attribute point higher than the maximum listed his phenotype (see the Attribute Maximums Table maximums attainable through this trait are shown in parentheses) Note this trait only increas es the maximum_ not the Attribute itself or the Attribute thresh old. Character Points must be spent tO purchase the Attribute as usual, at the usual cost: This trait can only be purchased one time per Attribute and character can only have one Exceptional Attribute unless the gamemaster gives specific permission otherwise. is acceptable for character to take this trait without increasing the Attribute during character creation, allowing that Attribute to be increased point beyond the maximum at later time through experience and training:
GLASS JAW Value: character with glass jaw' is frail and easy t0 injure, out of proportion with his endurance ad the other effects of the BOD Attribute_ such, the wound value of all wounds increased by against character with this trait. This increas the wound value   only; the wound  type (such minor) remains the same For example_ Minor Wound against char- acter with Glass Jaw has wound value of 3 rather than 2; it iS still a Minor Wound just higher value Note this has effect on the Fatigue Only result (see Damage; p 114).
GOOD HEARING Cost: 1 This trait makes the character'$ sense of hearing sharper than usual, enough to provide TN modifier to all hearing: based Perception Checks
FAST LEARNER Cost: 3 fast learner is quick study and has an excellent memo ry, resulting in faster skill improvement: Add +2 t0 the total of the two Link   Attributes when determining the cost  of   skill advancement (P 211) for all skills
GOOD REPUTATION Cost: 1-5 character with good reputation is known far and wide and this reputation can open doors and create opportunities (although it can also make it difficult to blend in' The point cost for this trait is determined by region in the same way as Bad Reputation (p 79). Note that this trait does not mean every single person in the area knows about the character; but rather that there is chance they might. Characters with the Good Reputation Trait receive moditier Negotiation Protocol and Seduction Checks in the affected region_ The gamemaster can also apply this modifier to other TNs as he deems appropriate.
FIGHTER PILOT PHENOTYPE Cost: Clan scientists have put considerable effort into the fight- pilot phenotype resulting in the most radical, but ironically least effective of the Clan trueborn strains. Generations refinement and sporadic genetic alteration of the pilot breed have resulted in warriors that are fairly short and very Iean
